steps.title: Match tense and subject
steps.content: "Even back in the 1860s" places the state in the past. The subject is "nearly 30% of the city's population," a singular quantity, so the verb needs singular past-tense agreement.

steps.title: Reject tense mismatches
steps.content: Choice A uses present progressive tense. Choice C uses simple present tense. Choice D uses future tense. The remaining verb is singular past tense and fits the 1860s time reference. The answer is B.
